skillName: Find the nth term of a sequence
misconceptionSummary: Students choose the correct coefficient from the common difference but pick the wrong adjustment term.

steps.narration: This rule is close, but it is wrong.
That gives seven for the first term, but the first term should be four.
Three n plus one matches the first term correctly.
Checking more terms confirms the rule works.

visuals.expression: Test n=1 to check the adjustment
visuals.label: A good nth term must match the actual sequence

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, test your rule on the first few terms.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: getting the coefficient right but the constant wrong. Always test the rule against the first term and at least one more term.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: A tiny error in the adjustment changes the whole sequence, so always verify the rule.
